skill.name: skill-developer
skill.description: Guide for creating and configuring Claude Code skills, hooks, agents, slash commands, and activation rules.
skill.summary: Create and manage Claude Code skill infrastructure with practical configuration guidance.
skill.author: 0Chan-smc

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 36 issues, but review found these are documentation examples and keyword matches inside SKILL.md. No executable code, network access, credential handling, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found. The low rating reflects documentation-only shell and filesystem examples that should still be reviewed when adapting the guidance.

low_findings.title: Documentation-Only External Command Matches
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The external command findings point to Markdown code fences, inline configuration terms, JSON examples, and one mkdir example. The skill does not contain executable scripts or instructions that automatically run shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced locations are visible Markdown examples and configuration snippets, not runtime code. There is no interpreter, script file, or user-controlled command construction in the skill.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are Keyword Collisions
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak cryptography findings appear to match ordinary words in descriptions and headings. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, encryption routine, or credential processing is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited lines shows documentation text, not cryptographic code. The file contains no calls to MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar weak algorithms.

low_findings.title: Hidden File Access Match Is A Path Exclusion Example
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The hidden path is a JSON example showing how to exclude a build output directory from skill triggers. It is not filesystem access and does not read or write hidden files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is inside a pathExclusions example for configuration matching. It does not perform hidden file enumeration, reading, writing, or deletion.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Match Is Guidance Text
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The system reconnaissance finding points to advice about avoiding over-triggering in skill configuration. No host, process, environment, or network reconnaissance behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is a best-practice sentence in documentation. It does not invoke system discovery commands or request sensitive local state.

content.user_title: Build Claude Code Skills and Hooks
content.value_statement: Teams often lack a consistent process for creating Claude Code skills and activation rules. This skill provides structured guidance for skills, hooks, agents, commands, and trigger configuration.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to create skills, hooks, agents, and slash commands.
Shows the expected directory structure for Claude Code skills.
Describes skill-rules.json fields for prompt and file triggers.
Documents enforcement levels, priority values, and trigger matching patterns.
Provides checklists for adding and troubleshooting skill activation.
limitations: It is a documentation guide and does not create files automatically.
It focuses on Claude Code conventions, not every AI assistant platform.
It does not validate configuration files or run integration tests.
It does not include advanced security policies for untrusted hooks.

use_cases.title: Create a Team Skill
use_cases.description: Define a new skill with clear triggers, purpose, and documentation structure.

use_cases.title: Tune Skill Activation
use_cases.description: Improve path, content, and prompt triggers so skills activate at the right time.

use_cases.title: Document Workflow Extensions
use_cases.description: Plan related hooks, agents, and commands before adding them to a project.

prompt_templates.title: Plan A Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a Claude Code skill for my project. Ask for the domain, trigger keywords, and file patterns before proposing the structure.

prompt_templates.title: Draft Trigger Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Create prompt and file trigger recommendations for a skill that should activate when developers work on the following area: [describe area].

prompt_templates.title: Review Skill Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this skill concept for over-triggering, missing exclusions, unclear enforcement level, and weak activation keywords.

prompt_templates.title: Design A Skill System
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a coordinated set of Claude Code skills, hooks, agents, and commands for this repository workflow. Include responsibilities and activation boundaries.

output_examples.input: I need a frontend skill that activates for React components.
output: A proposed skill purpose focused on frontend component work.
Recommended prompt keywords for component, React, and UI tasks.
Suggested file patterns for component directories and React files.
Exclusions for tests, build output, and dependency folders.

output_examples.input: My skill is triggering too often.
output: A review of broad keywords that may cause noisy activation.
More specific intent patterns for the desired workflow.
Path exclusions for generated files and unrelated directories.

output_examples.input: I want to add hooks and slash commands around a release process.
output: A separation of responsibilities between documentation, hooks, agents, and commands.
Recommended enforcement levels for advisory and blocking behavior.
A checklist for testing activation before rollout.

best_practices: Start with a narrow skill purpose and expand only after real usage shows gaps.
Use specific prompt triggers and path exclusions to reduce noisy activation.
Keep long reference material in separate resources so the main skill stays focused.
anti_patterns: Do not use broad keywords that trigger for unrelated development tasks.
Do not make every skill blocking unless the workflow truly requires enforcement.
Do not place large, unrelated guides in a single SKILL.md file.

faq.question: Does this skill create new skills automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance, examples, and checklists for creating and configuring skills manually.

faq.question: Can I use it with Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What is the main audience?
faq.answer: It is for developers who maintain Claude Code workflows, skill rules, hooks, agents, and commands.

faq.question: Does it include security review guidance?
faq.answer: It includes general structure guidance, but it does not provide a full security audit process.

faq.question: Why does it mention hidden folders?
faq.answer: It shows hidden build output paths as exclusions, so skills do not activate on generated files.

faq.question: Can it help reduce noisy skill activation?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains trigger specificity, path exclusions, priority levels, and over-triggering troubleshooting.
